title: Implement SearchDeduplicator with configurable cooldown for autonomy
description:
## Source
Council review at `Data/Pages/council/blueprint-yaml-autonomy-council-2026-06-29.md` — Retrieval Specialist finding  
Autonomy Phase 1 prerequisite

## Background
Timer-driven autonomy wakeups could cause the LLM evaluator to trigger multiple `IMemoryGateway.SearchAsync` calls. Current search has zero deduplication or caching — every call makes an HTTP request to MemorySmith. Without suppression, timer wakes could produce 12+ searches/minute with identical queries.

## Scope
1. Implement `SearchDeduplicator` component that:
   - Normalizes search queries (lowercase, trimmed, whitespace-collapsed)
   - Computes content hash
   - Suppresses identical queries within `SearchCooldownSeconds` (default 30s)
   - Logs every suppressed search with hash and original query
2. Integrate into `RestMemoryGateway.SearchAsync` (or as decorator)
3. Add configurable cooldown in `RestMemoryGatewayOptions`
4. Add `MaxSearchesPerMinute` global rate limit (default 10)

## Design constraints
- All suppressed searches must log with reason (Rule E-3 compliance — no silent dropping)
- Cooldown must be configurable via appsettings.json
- Rate limit must be testable with fake time

## Deliverables
- `SearchDeduplicator` class
- Integration into `RestMemoryGateway`
- Configuration options
- Unit tests: deduplication, cooldown enforcement, rate limiting
- Debug log for each suppressed query

## Exit Criteria
- Identical queries within cooldown window are suppressed
- Suppression logged with hash and original query
- Max 10 searches/minute enforced
- Configurable via appsettings.json
- All existing search tests pass
